name: Delta N_eff from dark radiation
required_observables: Delta N_eff estimate for 13 bosonic dof + thermal history
missing_observables: Dark sector temperature ratio xi=T_D/T_SM at BBN/CMB, Thermalization history
conventions: Bound: Delta N_eff < 0.18 (two-sided, 95% CL) or <0.226 (one-sided). Planck-only: <0.28. BBN-only: <0.74.
applicability: Affects full PTA-preferred region if xi > 0.4. But minimal model has no SM coupling for thermalization.
tested_subregion: Parameter: xi = T_D/T_SM. Condition: affected if xi > (0.18/0.64)^{1/4} ~ 0.4.
notes: Bringmann et al. (2306.09411) found stable dark sectors strongly disfavored for PTA. Need to compute FOPT reheating to dark sector vs SM.
source_title: Probing Physics Beyond the Standard Model: Limits from BBN and the CMB Independently and Combined

name: BBN bound on percolation/reheating temperature
missing_observables: Dependence on whether dark sector energy goes to SM or stays in DS
conventions: Bound: T_reh > 3 MeV for alpha~1 (Bai & Korwar 2022). Minimal: T_p > 1 MeV.
applicability: Best 14/14 points (T_reh~40-50 MeV) safe. MAP point (T_reh~14.5 MeV) safe. Some low-T_p scan points marginal.
tested_subregion: Parameter: T_p. Condition: T_p > 1 MeV (minimal), T_reh > 3 MeV for strong transitions. Full PTA-preferred region safe.
notes: Even in secluded sector, FOPT energy affects Hubble rate during BBN. Already computed in pipeline.
source_title: BBN constraints on MeV-scale phase transitions

name: Dark matter overproduction from stable gauge bosons
required_observables: Relic abundance for m_W~0.26-0.58 GeV, g~0.86
missing_observables: Annihilation cross section, Boltzmann solution, portal coupling (if any)
applicability: Full PTA-preferred region affected. Gauge bosons stable with custodial symmetry; standard freeze-out vastly overproduces.
tested_subregion: Parameter: portal coupling. Condition: overproduction unless freeze-in via tiny portal (lambda_2~10^{-9} like Borah et al.) or efficient annihilations.
notes: Frandsen et al. (2301.00041): minimal SU(2) VDM requires m_V > 7.5 TeV for standard freeze-out. Sub-GeV case requires freeze-in.

name: Self-interacting dark matter constraints (structure formation)
observable: sigma_T/m (transfer cross section per unit mass)
required_observables: Transfer cross section for W^+W^- scattering at relevant velocities
missing_observables: Non-perturbative sigma_T/m calculation including Sommerfeld enhancement
conventions: Cluster bound: sigma/m < 0.35 cm^2/g. Bullet Cluster: <1.25 cm^2/g. Dwarf needed: 1-10 cm^2/g.
applicability: If gauge bosons are DM, self-interaction expected to be strong (sigma ~ g^4/m_W^2).
tested_subregion: Parameter: DM fraction f_DM. Condition: constrained only if W bosons are DM (f_DM > ~0.01).
notes: Sub-leading to C03 (DM overproduction). Need relic abundance calculation first.
source_title: Velocity-dependent Self-interacting Dark Matter from Groups and Clusters of Galaxies

name: Fifth force / equivalence principle constraints on light scalar
missing_observables: Mixing angle with SM Higgs (zero in conformal limit)
conventions: MICROSCOPE: eta < 2 x 10^{-15} for m < 10^{-12} eV. Cassini: |gamma-1| < 2.3 x 10^{-5}.
applicability: NOT APPLICABLE: (1) m_chi ~ MeV too heavy for lab tests (range ~10^{-11} cm). (2) Conformal dilaton has no SM coupling at tree level.
tested_subregion: Parameter: m_chi. Condition: not applicable for any PTA-preferred point (m_chi > 10^{-12} eV).
notes: Damour-Donoghue (2010): purely spontaneous scale breaking gives zero coupling to matter.
source_title: Equivalence Principle Violations and Couplings of a Light Dilaton

name: Perturbativity bound on gauge coupling
applicability: Trivially satisfied. PTA-preferred g in [0.85,0.91] well within perturbative regime.
tested_subregion: Parameter: g. Condition: g < 3.5. Full region satisfies.
notes: Needed for one-loop CW calculation validity.

name: CMB constraints on dark matter annihilation
required_observables: Annihilation cross section to SM, branching ratios, energy deposition efficiency
missing_observables: Annihilation cross section to SM (requires portal), f_c for MeV-GeV DM
conventions: Planck: p_eff < 3.2 x 10^{-28} cm^3/s/GeV (Slatyer 2016). Thermal target: <sigma v> ~ 3 x 10^{-26} cm^3/s.
applicability: Applies only if gauge bosons are DM with s-wave annihilation to SM via a portal. Minimal model lacks this portal.
tested_subregion: Parameter: portal coupling and DM mass. Condition: constrains if Omega_W ~ Omega_DM and has SM annihilation channel.
notes: Borah et al. freeze-in (lambda_2~10^{-9}) gives negligible annihilation, evading CMB constraints.
source_title: Indirect dark matter signatures in the cosmic dark ages

name: Supernova cooling constraints on light dark sector particles
observable: Energy loss rate in proto-neutron star
required_observables: Production cross section in SN core, trapping optical depth, portal coupling
missing_observables: Portal coupling to SM (determines production), scattering cross section in nuclear matter
applicability: Gauge bosons (m_W~0.26-0.58 GeV) heavier than T_SN~30 MeV: production Boltzmann-suppressed. Radial mode (m_chi~38-89 MeV) marginal.
tested_subregion: Parameter: m and portal coupling. Condition: constrained only if m < T_SN AND portal exists. m_chi~38-89 MeV near/above T_SN.
notes: Chang et al. (2017 JHEP) detailed SN bounds on dark photons. Caputo et al. (2025 PRL) new shock-revival constraints for m~0.1-0.4 MeV.
source_title: Supernova bounds on the dark photon using its electromagnetic decay

name: Dark sector thermalization: Higgs portal constraints
observable: Higgs invisible BR, DM-nucleon cross section, collider production
missing_observables: Portal coupling (not in minimal model), mixing angle
conventions: ATLAS: BR(h->inv) < 0.11 (95% CL). LZ: sigma_SI < 10^{-10} pb for m_DM~30 GeV.
applicability: Minimal model has NO portal. Borah et al. add lambda_2~10^{-9}, safely below all constraints. Larger portal for decays would be constrained.
tested_subregion: Parameter: portal coupling. Condition: for lambda_2~10^{-9} all constraints evaded. Larger portals need evaluation.
notes: Thermalization rate Gamma ~ lambda_2^2 T. lambda_2 < 10^{-6} prevents thermalization.
source_title: A first order dark SU(2)_D phase transition with vector dark matter

name: Eternal inflation bound on supercooling
observable: Gamma/H^4 (bubble nucleation rate per Hubble volume)
conventions: Condition: Gamma/H^4 > 1 to avoid eternal inflation (Guth 2007)
applicability: Already checked by FOPT pipeline. PTA-preferred region survives. 14/1660 low-chi0 scan points excluded.
tested_subregion: Parameter: Gamma/H^4. Condition: Gamma/H^4 > 1 required. PTA-preferred region safe.
notes: Eternal inflation filter applied to FOPT scan. All MAP and best-fit points satisfy this.

name: Dark photon/hidden photon constraints from beam dump experiments
required_observables: Kinetic mixing or portal strength, production cross section
missing_observables: Kinetic mixing epsilon (not in minimal model), effective coupling of SU(2) triplet to EM current
conventions: BaBar: epsilon < 10^{-3} for m=0.02-10 GeV. NA64: epsilon < 3x10^{-4} for m<1 GeV. LSND: epsilon < 10^{-3} to 3x10^{-2} for m=10-300 MeV.
applicability: Only relevant if kinetic mixing portal is added to the model. Minimal model has no mixing. Non-Abelian nature requires recast.
tested_subregion: Parameter: kinetic mixing epsilon. Condition: for epsilon > 10^{-4}-10^{-3}, m_W~260-580 MeV constrained by BaBar/NA64.
notes: Belle II and LDMX will probe smaller mixing. Only relevant for model extensions with a decay portal.
source_title: Search for Invisible Decays of a Dark Photon Produced in e+e- Collisions at BaBar
